bindings.quest_action: follow the tracks and bring my daughter home
bindings.quest_focus: the trail of heavy boots and dragging marks into the treeline
bindings.quest_stakes: a ten-year-old girl taken from her bed in the night
bindings.quest_threat: the abductors who snatched her while we slept
bindings.quest_time_pressure: every second they're getting further away

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}! Please, stop! My child is gone and {quest_time_pressure}!
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} took her last night. Right out of the house. {target_npc} heard the door close and ran out, but all they saw were {enemy_type} shapes disappearing into the treeline {location_hint}. {quest_focus} are clear: she's alive. The guards are organizing a search, but they're too slow. I need you to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_stakes} is out there in the dark with monsters. Please. I will give you everything I own.

playerResponses.text: Keep your possessions. Just show me the tracks and I'll bring her home.
I'll follow the trail now. Don't lose hope.
I'm deeply sorry, but tracking at night through hostile territory is beyond me.

bindings.quest_action: find where the vanished are being taken and stop the abductions
bindings.quest_focus: the strange organized tracks leading away from empty beds
bindings.quest_stakes: three people who vanished without a trace
bindings.quest_threat: a methodical force selecting and spiriting people away

dialogueChunks.intro: Something is taking people, {player_name}. Not killing them: taking them. And nobody knows where they go.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_stakes} in the last two weeks. No bodies, no blood, no signs of struggle: just empty beds. A hunter found {quest_focus} {location_hint}: {enemy_type} tracks in lines and formations. {quest_threat} is selecting people and spiriting them away.
dialogueChunks.outro: The settlement is terrified. Doors are barred every night. We need you to {quest_action} before the next bed turns up empty.

playerResponses.text: Organized abductions? This isn't random. I'll follow the tracks and find the pattern.
Three people vanished without a trace? That's unsettling. I'll investigate.
Whatever is doing this is methodical and dangerous. I'd rather not be next.

bindings.quest_action: track the abductors and rescue the captive
bindings.quest_focus: the market square where they struck in broad daylight
bindings.quest_stakes: a settlement that no longer feels safe even in daylight
bindings.quest_threat: raiders bold enough to snatch someone from a crowded market
bindings.quest_time_pressure: before they disappear into the deep wilderness

dialogueChunks.intro: I wasn't going to involve outsiders, {player_name}. But we've run out of options and time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} was taken in broad daylight from {quest_focus}: a group of {enemy_type} stormed in, grabbed them, and were gone before anyone could draw a weapon. {quest_threat} headed {location_hint} with a speed that suggests they knew exactly where they were going. The guards lost the trail at the river.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_stakes}. They're not even afraid of us anymore. {quest_action} before {quest_time_pressure}.

playerResponses.text: Taken in daylight? They're getting bold. I'll pick up the trail at the river.
A coordinated snatch in a crowded market? These aren't ordinary raiders.
If they can grab someone from a crowded square, nowhere is safe. I'm leaving.

bindings.quest_action: rescue the hostages before the deadline expires
bindings.quest_focus: the enemy stronghold where they're being held
bindings.quest_stakes: five hostages facing mutilation if the ransom isn't met
bindings.quest_threat: captors who nailed a ransom demand in blood to our gate
bindings.quest_time_pressure: five days before they start sending back fingers

dialogueChunks.intro: They left a ransom demand, {player_name}. Nailed to the gate in blood.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} are holding {target_npc} and four others in {quest_focus} {location_hint}. {quest_threat} demands a cartload of {quest_item} within {quest_time_pressure}. The settlement doesn't have what they're asking for. We need someone to {quest_action}: either rescue them directly or gather enough {quest_item} to negotiate.

playerResponses.text: I'll get the hostages out before the deadline. No ransom needed.
Five days is tight but workable. I'll gather what I can and plan a rescue.
Ransom demands backed by mutilation threats? This is too dangerous.
